preamble: The shift from a voiced palatal plosive (/ɟ/) to a palatalized voiced alveolar plosive (/dʲ/) represents a common phonetic transformation, often involving a re-articulation of the primary place of articulation while retaining a secondary palatal quality. This change is observed in various language families, particularly those with extensive palatalization processes.
phoneticEffects: Depalatalization of primary articulation, retention of secondary palatalization, lenition, place assimilation

examples.note: This shift occurred as a lenition process in Common Slavic, though it did not affect all dialects, such as Novgorod-Pskov.

sources: Source via Google Search grounding (verify before merge)
